specVersion: JCVI/UKHSA 2026/27 season (2026-06-20 Keeper run: flu homelessness 16+, shingles immunosuppressed 18+, PCV20/RSV notes; 2026-06-22 CSO-signed-off all pending items)
notes: Seasonal rules (flu, COVID) use schedule+season for campaign-window suppression. One-off lifetime rules (pneumococcal, shingles, RSV) use schedule:once. INTENTIONAL OMISSIONS: pertussis-in-pregnancy and RSV-in-pregnancy are not encoded — the engine has no per-pregnancy episode gate or gestational-age gate; a lifetime lookback cannot distinguish doses given in prior pregnancies.

rules.source: JCVI/UKHSA Green Book Chapter 19 (Influenza). 2026/27 programme carries forward the 2025/26 eligible groups, age bands and risk groups; a people-experiencing-homelessness cohort aged 16+ is added from 1 Oct 2026 per the Amendment to National Flu Immunisation Programme 2026 to 2027 letter (DHSC/NHSE/UKHSA, published 9 June 2026 — WebSearch-corroborated, ageMin:16 applied 2026-06-20 by The Keeper).
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY: Status inferred from coded records only — vaccination given elsewhere may not appear. Asthma sub-criterion requires inhaled/systemic steroids or prior hospital admission (proxy via medication check). Carers and care home residents are not detected. Homelessness is rarely coded in GP records, so the homelessness cohort flags only patients with an explicit code — not the broader population; operational outreach remains the primary route. BMI reading may be out of date.

rules.source: JCVI statement on COVID-19 vaccination in autumn 2026 and spring 2027, published 16 July 2025 (gov.uk). Confirmed: same 3 cohorts as 2025 — age 75+, care home residents, and immunosuppressed ≥6 months. UK government acceptance confirmed 23 March 2026. WebSearch-corroborated 2026-06-20 by The Keeper.
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY: As of 2025/26, clinical risk groups (diabetes, CHD, CKD, asthma) are NO LONGER eligible for COVID booster — only age 75+, care home residents, and immunosuppressed. Status inferred from coded records only — vaccination given elsewhere may not appear.

rules.source: UKHSA Green Book Chapter 25 (Pneumococcal) — single dose at age 65+; the routine adult vaccine changed from PPV23 to PCV20 (Prevenar 20, Pfizer; previously marketed as Apexxnar in EU — UK product name is Prevenar 20) in early 2026 (CSO-approved 2026-06-17; verified against NHS England regional communications 2026-06-20 by The Keeper). No routine revaccination.
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY: One-off dose from age 65 (routine vaccine is now PCV20; a previously-recorded PPV23 dose still counts as given). Status inferred from coded records only — vaccination given elsewhere (or pre-registration) may not appear. Revaccination every 5 years applies ONLY to asplenia/splenic dysfunction/CKD and is NOT encoded here — check manually for those patients. At-risk under-65 cohort (Green Book ch 25 risk groups) intentionally not encoded. Rule id retained as 'vax-pneumo-ppv23' so existing user snoozes/overrides are preserved.

rules.source: UKHSA Green Book Chapter 28a (Shingles) — phased programme from 1 Sept 2023: routine offer on turning 65 (cohort born on/after 1 Sept 1958) plus existing 70–79 cohort; Shingrix 2 doses 6–12 months apart
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY AND DOSES: Shingrix is a 2-dose course (6–12 months apart) — this chip shows GIVEN on ANY recorded shingles/zoster vaccination and CANNOT verify 2-dose completion; check dose count manually. People aged 68–69 who turned 65 before 1 Sept 2023 are NOT yet eligible and are correctly not flagged. Severely immunosuppressed pathway expanded from Sept 2025 to include all ages 18+ (UKHSA/NHSE expansion letter published 22 July 2025, effective 1 September 2025 — CSO-signed-off 2026-06-22). Historic Zostavax counts as given. MEDICATION NOTE: Methotrexate (>20mg/week) and azathioprine (>3mg/kg/day) also qualify for the severely immunosuppressed cohort but have dose thresholds the engine cannot verify — patients on these drugs must be assessed manually rather than flagged automatically; see medication criterion below.

anyOf.label: Severely immunosuppressed 18+ (medication — Sept 2025 expansion, CSO-signed-off 2026-06-22; methotrexate/azathioprine excluded — dose-threshold check required)

rules.source: UKHSA Green Book Chapter 27a (RSV) / JCVI advice — routine offer from age 75; eligibility expanded from 1 April 2026 to all adults aged 80+ and adult care-home residents (CSO-approved 2026-06-17; confirm against the JCVI/NHSE expansion letter).
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY: Single dose, no booster currently recommended. Eligibility expanded from 1 April 2026 — specifically: (1) adults aged 80 and over added (previously 75–79 only; now no upper age limit from 75+); (2) residents in care homes for older adults added regardless of age. Source: NHSE/UKHSA expansion letter published 2 February 2026, effective 1 April 2026 — WebSearch-corroborated by The Keeper 2026-06-20 (gov.uk, Care Provider Alliance, Nursing in Practice). RSV-in-pregnancy pathway (from 28 weeks) is intentionally NOT encoded — see pertussis omission note.
